overview.pattern: Declarative GitOps-style infrastructure repo for a k3s staging cluster
overview.description: Source of truth for Solid Stats staging runtime wiring. Owns Kubernetes manifests, observability stack, host edge config, operational scripts, and backup/restore runbooks. Application repos own source and images; this repo composes those images into a reliable runtime. No application source or HTTP API lives here.

data-flow.flow: push to master -> GitHub Actions -> ssh-tunnel-up.sh (local-forward to closed k3s API) -> kubeconfig-setup.sh (SA token) -> render secrets -> kubectl apply k8s/** -> verify rollouts
data-flow.description: Two independent CD pipelines: runtime (solid-stats-staging) and observability (monitoring + error-tracking). The k3s API is firewalled closed; all CI access flows through a fail-closed SSH local-forward gate.

conventions.naming: Numeric filename prefixes (00-, 10-, 20-...) define kubectl apply ordering. nginx vhosts named <subdomain>-solid-stats.conf.
conventions.file-organization: One environment per directory (k8s/staging, k8s/observability). values/ + dashboards/ are helm/template inputs, not directly applied. config/ holds host-side nginx/systemd/s3 artifacts.
conventions.imports: Scripts are #!/usr/bin/env bash with set -euo pipefail; Python is stdlib-only. Required env vars checked via parameter expansion / required() helper; exit 64 on missing config.
conventions.secrets: No secret values in git. Secrets rendered from GitHub environment secrets at deploy time via render-*-secrets.py; trap-removed temp files; split per namespace for scoped tokens.

deployment-boundary.operator-vs-ci: Five manifests are operator-bootstrapped and excluded from CD globs: 00-namespace, 01-ci-rbac, 01-obs-rbac, 02-priority-classes, 03-alloy-rbac (cluster-scoped or RBAC the CI tokens cannot create).
deployment-boundary.description: CI uses least-privilege scoped ServiceAccount tokens: ci-deployer (runtime), obs-ci-deployer (monitoring), obs-ci-deployer-et (error-tracking, cannot touch monitoring).

validation.strategy: Offline static validators (validate-staging.py, validate-obs-manifests.py, validate-*-edge.py, validate-s3-lifecycle.py) run in CI with no cluster access; live assertion harnesses (validate-phase-12/13/15/16.sh, validate-stack.sh) run post-apply against the cluster.
validation.description: Completion claims require fresh evidence from scripts, kubectl dry-runs, rollout state, backup logs, or S3 checks.

safety-gates.backup-gate: start-controlled-full-run.sh refuses unless docs/backup-gate.md is 'Status: verified'.
safety-gates.cutover-gate: cutover.sh enforces backup-gate verified + diff-readiness strict_failures:0 before any nginx upstream switch; reversible with auto-rollback and DRY_RUN/SELF_TEST modes.
safety-gates.replays-fetcher: Kept suspended until manual backup and restore-list validation pass.

component:Runtime workloads.responsibility: PostgreSQL (17) + RabbitMQ (4) StatefulSets, server-2 / web / replay-parser-2 Deployments, replays-fetcher + postgres-backup CronJobs.

component:Observability metrics.responsibility: Prometheus, kube-state-metrics, node-exporter, postgres-exporter, Grafana + dashboards, monitoring NetworkPolicy. Pods carry priorityClassName obs-background.

component:Observability logs.responsibility: Loki log store fed by Grafana Alloy DaemonSet; Grafana logs dashboard.

component:Error tracking.responsibility: GlitchTip web/worker + dedicated PostgreSQL + migrate/seed Jobs in error-tracking namespace, with its own NetworkPolicy and scoped CI token.

component:Host edge.responsibility: VPS nginx vhosts (stats, grafana, errors subdomains), certbot/Let's Encrypt TLS with renewal hooks + failure alerting, ufw rules, reversible production cutover. Adopt-reconcile idempotent bootstrap run as root on the VPS.

component:Backup and DR.responsibility: Scheduled PostgreSQL dumps to Timeweb S3 (backups/postgres/), one-off manual backup, restore drill, and S3 lifecycle retention.
